plan.executiveSummary: Balanced recovery meets the Day-21 target while preserving meaningful budget headroom and avoiding the speed plan's overtime concentration.
plan.strategyRationale: The balanced strategy reaches 82.2% simulated OTIF for $68,750, remains inside every hard constraint, and distributes recovery across approved supplier allocation, production resequencing, and bounded carrier capacity.

assumptions.statement: The approved alternate supplier covers 38% of the Atlas shortage.
Carrier Delta pickup acceptance reaches 92% by Day 7.

crossTeamPriorities.priority: Resequence feasible strategic orders at Wilmington.
Reserve bounded Carrier Delta recovery capacity.

residualRisks: Carrier acceptance may remain one point below the simulated assumption.
Contributor shares remain observed associations rather than exclusive causal proof.

findings.title: Carrier assumption exceeds observed evidence
findings.detail: The plan assumes 92% pickup acceptance while the deterministic observation reaches 91%; retain the Day-7 checkpoint and variance disclosure.

findings.title: Causality language is appropriately bounded
findings.detail: The plan treats contributor shares as observed associations and does not claim exclusive causal identification.

requiredConditions: Retain explicit human approval before any material synthetic operation.
Measure and disclose the Day-21 projection variance.

counterfactual.scenario: Carrier pickup acceptance remains at 88% through Day 14.
counterfactual.expectedImpact: Day-21 OTIF would likely close below the 82.0% target, requiring a bounded revision rather than silent escalation.
